name: Applying for Scholarships
text:
Applying for Scholarships -- BYU university scholarships and financial aid (Enrollment Services).
BYU scholarships are available for
current students and for those applying for admission
. To be considered for any scholarship offered through the Financial Aid Office, you must complete the scholarship application.
You
must
apply for scholarships on or before the posted deadlines,
even if you have not yet been admitted to BYU
. To be considered for scholarships after admission, you must apply by the
Continuing Student Deadline
each year.
To receive full consideration for all scholarships for which you may be eligible, you must complete the FAFSA (Free Application for Federal Student Aid) and submit it to BYU each year by
January 2
.
Application Deadlines & Notifications
NOTE:
Deadlines listed also apply to international students. Graduate students must contact their department for applications and deadlines.
Winter 2027
Spring & Summer 2027
Fall 2027
New Freshman Deadline
New Freshman Notification
Aug 1, 2026
End of August
Jan 2, 2027
End of February
Jan 2, 2027
End of February
Former Deadline
Former Notification
Aug 1, 2026
End of August
Jan 2, 2027
End of February
Jan 2, 2027
End of February
Continuing Deadline
Continuing Notification
Aug 1, 2026*
End of August
Jan 2, 2027
End of February
Jan 2, 2027
End of February
Transfer Deadline **
Transfer Notification
Aug 1, 2026
End of August
Jan 2, 2027
N/A
Jan 2, 2027
End of February
* The winter Continuing Student Deadline is for return missionaries who are returning in the winter semester. For more information about applying for winter 2027 scholarships, see "For Winter 2027 Scholarship Applicants" below.
**
Transfer students
can apply for
academic scholarships
as Continuing Students after completing at least 12 BYU credit hours on or before the January 2 Scholarship Application Deadline.
Additional Information
Toggle Item
For Winter 2027 Scholarship Applicants
The winter 2027 scholarship application is available to students entering or returning to BYU in winter semester. This includes:
New freshmen
Readmitted former students
New transfer students
Missionaries returning in winter semester
The scholarship application deadline is
August 1
.
To receive full consideration for all winter scholarships you may be eligible for, you must complete the 2026-2027
FAFSA
by
July 20
.
Toggle Item
ACT/SAT for Freshman Academic Scholarships
If you want to be considered for Presidential or multi-year merit scholarships, you should opt in to allow BYU to use the
ACT/SAT score
on your admission application. When we begin scholarship awarding, we will consider the highest ACT/SAT score sent to BYU. If you submit an admission application with your ACT/SAT score and then later receive a higher ACT/SAT score, you only need to
send the new score
to BYU. You do not need to resubmit your admission application (this is for scholarship consideration purposes only).
Some merit-based scholarships
do not
require the ACT/SAT, so you will still be considered for those scholarships without the score if you do not submit it.
Toggle Item
FAFSA & Scholarships
If you are considered a
dependent student
on the FAFSA, your parent information will be required for scholarship consideration.
We will consider independent students for all applicable scholarships, even though you cannot include parent information on the FAFSA. However, we will use other need-based components available to us to determine need. Please note:
A FAFSA is required for
any
student who wants to be considered for need-based scholarships.
We use multiple factors available to us to award need-based scholarships to students.
Toggle Item
For Departing/Returning Missionaries
A Leave of Absence may be taken directly prior to a mission deferment. Following your mission, you must enroll at BYU within a designated time period, as noted in the
Mission Deferment Form
. Fall/winter scholarships awarded to you before you depart will be deferred during this time period Spring/summer scholarships may not be deferred for any reason.
Your scholarship will automatically be reactivated for the semester/term you are scheduled to re-enroll at BYU. The following information about each scholarship applies:
Freshman Scholarships-
Apply for freshman scholarships during your senior year of high school by the appropriate deadline listed above, even if you plan to serve a mission before attending BYU.
Continuing Student Academic Scholarships-
To apply as a Continuing Student for academic scholarships either before, during, or after your mission, you must have completed at least 12 BYU credits before the February 1 deadline. If you have earned the 12 credits and completed a Mission Deferment Form, you will automatically be considered for an academic scholarship for the semester you return. You are not required to submit a new scholarship application while on your mission unless you want to be considered for additional scholarships.
Financial Need-Based Scholarships-
You must submit the FAFSA for the upcoming year by the January 20 deadline. You are not required to submit a new scholarship application when returning from your mission. However, if you have special circumstances, you should fill out the scholarship application by the appropriate deadline listed above and complete the essay in the 'Need' section of the application.
Toggle Item
For Graduate Students
Contact your department for scholarship information.
Toggle Item
For International Students
Scholarships for international students are very limited through the
Financial Aid Office
. Contact
International Student & Scholar Services
for more opportunities.
For scholarships through the Financial Aid Office, complete the scholarship application by the deadline listed above.
Toggle Item
Scholarship FAQs
See our
Scholarship FAQs
page for more information about applying for scholarships.

name: Eligibility for Scholarships
text:
Eligibility for Scholarships -- BYU university scholarships and financial aid (Enrollment Services).
Toggle Item
Minimum Credit Hour Requirement
Maintain the number of credits the scholarship requires, typically 12 for fall/winter.
Independent Study courses, audited courses, and language exam credits do not count.
Scholarships will not be credited to your account until you register for the appropriate number of credit hours.
Toggle Item
Scholarship Thank You Letter Requirement
To receive scholarship funds awarded by the Financial Aid Office, you must submit
one thank you letter per academic year
. Completing this letter is required before your scholarship can be applied to your My Financial Center. For example:
Students who start in the
fall semester
should submit their letter prior to the fall Add/Drop Deadline.
Students who start in the
winter semester
should submit their letter prior to the winter Add/Drop Deadline.
If you receive a scholarship for the next academic year, you will submit another thank you letter at that time.
Deadline
The thank you letter must be submitted by the
add/drop deadline
during the first semester of each scholarship.
If you do not submit your thank you letter by the applicable deadline, your scholarship will be canceled.
Missionary/Military Deferment
If you are planning to defer your scholarship for missionary service, you must submit a deferment request by the end of the semester the scholarship was awarded. If you plan to defer for military service, you must
submit a case
to the Financial Aid Office with your military orders.
If you do not submit your deferment letter by the applicable deadline, your scholarship will be canceled.
Where to Submit
Complete and submit your thank you letter electronically on your Scholarship Summary page in
My Financial Center
.
Who Needs to Complete This
All students awarded a BYU scholarship.
If you are deferring your scholarship for missionary service, you may submit the letter when you return.
Departmental Scholarships
If you received a departmental scholarship, deadlines may differ. Contact your department for more information.
Toggle Item
Dropping and Withdrawing from Classes
If you fall below the minimum credit hour requirement at any point, your scholarship will be canceled, and you will be responsible for any tuition charges and/or withdrawal fees.
If
unusual or extenuating circumstances
require you to drop or withdraw from individual classes or all classes in a semester, please visit an Enrollment Services Counselor in advance, if possible.
Toggle Item
Using Your Scholarship
Scholarships are awarded for specific semesters. You may not combine the amount awarded for two semesters into one. Also, you may not move unclaimed amounts from one semester to another except for deferring for a full-time church mission or active duty military service. However, if you are awarded for two semesters, you may decline the scholarship for one semester without negating the other.
Toggle Item
Eligibility Limits
The Financial Aid Office limits individual eligibility for scholarships to eight semesters of fall/winter scholarship per student.
Spring/summer scholarships do not count against the eight-semester limit.
All scholarships awarded by the Financial Aid Office are for undergraduate students. You will need to contact your department/college for graduate scholarship opportunities.
Toggle Item
Leave of Absence
Continuing students on a
Leave of Absence
for reasons other than a missionary deferment must apply by the appropriate deadline to be considered for scholarships upon their return, even if they are not currently enrolled.
Toggle Item
Taxation
Funds awarded as scholarships, fellowships, benefits, and grants are excluded from the recipient’s gross income to the extent that they are used for required tuition, books, fees, supplies, and equipment. Funds exceeding these expenses or used for other purposes, including room, board, research, and travel, must be included as taxable income.
Because you are responsible for taxes due to the government, keep receipts, cancelled checks, and other documents to detail qualified expenditures.
Consult your personal tax advisor with any questions or concerns.
Toggle Item
Multi-Year Scholarship Policies
In addition to the above policies and expectations, multi-year scholarships have the following:
Maintaining Your Scholarship
For multi-year scholarships awarded beginning with the 2024-2025 academic year, you must earn and maintain a minimum BYU cumulative GPA of 3.70 through the end of each winter semester.
For multi-year scholarships awarded before the 2024-2025 academic year, you must earn and maintain a minimum BYU cumulative GPA of 3.60 through the end of each winter semester.
As long as you maintain the required GPA, you do not need to complete the general scholarship application unless you wish to apply for a spring-summer scholarship. Note that spring-summer scholarships do not count toward your eight semesters of scholarship eligibility.
You are expected to enroll at BYU for the equivalent of two semesters each academic year unless serving a Latter-day Saint mission. If you miss a major semester, you can move the scholarship to spring and summer terms of the same academic year. If you do not plan on attending BYU during spring or summer terms, you would decline that portion of the scholarship.
Attending Spring/Summer
You may apply to receive spring-summer scholarships in addition to your multi-year award, although you may not claim both during the same enrollment.
To be considered for these additional awards, you must submit the
online scholarship application
by the
January 2 deadline
.

name: Mission Deferments for Scholarships
text:
Mission Deferments for Scholarships -- BYU university scholarships and financial aid (Enrollment Services).
You may defer fall/winter scholarships to serve a mission. Spring/summer scholarships are non-deferrable.
A leave of absence may be taken directly prior to a missionary deferment. Following your mission, you must enroll at BYU within a designated time period, as noted in the
Mission Deferment Form
. Fall/winter scholarships awarded to you before you depart will be deferred during this time period. Spring/summer scholarships may not be deferred for any reason.
Your scholarship will be available beginning the semester you return to BYU. If your scholarship is awarded as a percentage of tuition, its value will be increased in proportion to the cost of tuition.
By completing the scholarship application or the missionary deferment form, you authorize the university to disclose the following information to scholarship donors (including the family or estate representative of deceased donors) and/or university agents for scholarship consideration, awarding purposes, communicating award decisions, and donor relation functions:
Information in the scholarship application, including your name, academic major, and hometown
The name of any award you have received and the amount of the award
Academic indicators
Demographic and other personal data, including the information accessible through Personal Information in MyBYU
Financial data from the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA), if provided
Other information required for specific scholarship considerations

name: Scholarship FAQs
text:
Scholarship FAQs -- BYU university scholarships and financial aid (Enrollment Services).
Application Process & Timing
Toggle Item
When does the scholarship application open and close?
Spring 2026, Summer 2026, Fall 2026, & Winter 2027 Scholarships
*Winter 2027 Scholarships Only
Application open: end of August - January 2
Application open: end of February - August 1
Who can apply:
Current BYU students
New freshmen
Readmitted former students
New transfer students
Who can apply:
New freshmen
Readmitted former students
New transfer students
*NOTE:
All applicants must be entering or returning to BYU in winter 2027.
Toggle Item
What if I miss the scholarship deadline?
Scholarship applications must be submitted by the posted deadlines. Late applications cannot be considered. However, once you have attended BYU, you can apply again the next year. You may also explore off-campus and departmental scholarships.
NOTE:
Continuing students are not eligible to apply for the winter 2027 scholarship application.
Toggle Item
Do I need to apply for scholarships every year, or is my application automatically renewed?
One-year scholarship recipients, Cougar Pell Promise, and Cougar Pell Elevated:
Must re-apply each year.
Multi-year scholarship recipients (including Presidential and Heritage):
Do not need to re-apply for major semesters (fall and winter) but must continue to meet program eligibility criteria to continue to receive subsequent awards. To be considered for spring/summer opportunities, you must submit a scholarship application for those terms.
You can check to see which scholarship you were awarded and the terms of the award in your notification letter.
Toggle Item
How do I know if my scholarship application went through?
Once you submit your scholarship application, you will receive a confirmation email sent to your BYU email. Check your inbox to make sure it goes through.
Toggle Item
Do I need to submit the FAFSA to be considered for BYU scholarships?
The FAFSA is only required to be considered for need-based scholarships, Cougar Pell Promise and Cougar Pell Elevated. It may also be required for some private scholarships. It is not required for merit scholarships such as the Presidential and Heritage scholarships.
Toggle Item
Is the scholarship application the same as the admissions application?
No, the scholarship application and admissions application are separate. To be considered for scholarships as a freshman, you must fill out the scholarship application. You will also need to apply for scholarships each year as a continuing student to be considered in the future.
Toggle Item
After I submit my application, can I still make changes?
Yes! Even if you have submitted your application, it will stay open, and you can continue to make edits up until the scholarship deadline. We will only review the version of your application that is submitted at that time.
Toggle Item
How do I get my scholarship money?
Getting your money from scholarships depends on the type of scholarship you received.
Types of Scholarships
BYU Scholarships:
Will automatically be applied to your student account to pay for tuition as long as you meet all
eligibility requirements
.
Off-Campus Scholarships:
Will be applied to your account soon after we receive a check from your scholarship donor, as long as you meet all
eligibility requirements
(off-campus scholarship eligibility requirements are different from BYU scholarship eligibility requirements). It typically takes 3-4 weeks for off-campus scholarships to be applied to your account.
Department Scholarships:
Contact the
college department
directly for information regarding that scholarship.
Excess Money from Scholarships
Excess scholarship funds (if any) will be refunded beginning on the 3rd day of classes. We recommend setting up
direct deposit
for faster delivery.
Applying for Scholarships
The deadline to
apply for scholarships
for the next academic year is
January 2
.
Eligibility & Requirements
Toggle Item
How are the essays on the application used?
Essays are not required to receive a scholarship - but they can really help. Your responses give us a better understanding of your experiences, which may open up more opportunities, especially for private or need-based awards.
Don't worry - we're not grading your grammar. We're simply looking for meaningful content that helps us get to know you better.
Toggle Item
What are the GPA cutoffs?
GPA cutoffs change from year to year. We allocate funds for one-year continuing student merit scholarships based on enrollment in each college. Then, your GPA is compared to other students in your college. Cutoffs vary based on the applicant pool and available funds.
Toggle Item
How is the ACT/SAT score used?
It is only used for freshmen students.
Although BYU is test optional, a score is required for Presidential and Heritage scholarships.
We use your ACT/SAT test score only if you opt-in to allow BYU to use a score on your admissions application. Simply providing a score to BYU does not mean we will automatically consider it in your scholarship application.
We will use the highest score provided to BYU by the scholarship application deadline. This differs from admissions policy.
If you opt in, your test score will be combined with your application info and ranked with other students to help decide your scholarship eligibility.
Toggle Item
What kind of scholarship opportunities are there?
There are several types of scholarships you can be considered for, depending on your background and academic performance. We encourage everyone to apply:
Presidential:
Eight semester scholarship awarded to 50 freshmen students each year. Awarded based on a holistic review.
Not available for the winter only awarding cycle.
Heritage:
Eight semester scholarship awarded to 200 freshmen students each year. Awarded based on a holistic review.
Cougar Pell Promise:
Eight semester tuition guarantee awarded to freshmen students who applied to the university immediately out of high school. Awarded based on information provided on the FAFSA.
Cougar Pell Elevated:
Eight semester tuition guarantee awarded to freshmen students who applied to the university immediately out of high school. Awarded to students with high financial need based on information provided on the FAFSA.
One Year Merit:
One year award based on the following academic criteria:
Freshmen:
holistic review
Current:
BYU cumulative GPA by college after a student has completed at least 12 BYU credit hours.
Private Donor Scholarships:
One year award based on specific donor criteria and essays.
Toggle Item
Who is eligible to apply for BYU scholarships?
Freshmen:
New students just admitted to BYU.
Eligible for: Presidential, Heritage, Cougar Pell Promise, Cougar Pell Elevated, one year merit, private donor scholarships
Current:
Students previously admitted and re-admitted students.
Eligible for: One year merit, one year need, private donor scholarships, need-based scholarships
Transfer:
New transfer students just admitted.
Eligible for: Private donor scholarships, merit scholarships after earning 12 BYU credit hours.
International:
Students who are not residents of the United States.
Eligible for: Private donor scholarships, merit scholarships after earning 12 BYU credit hours.
Graduate:
Students admitted into a graduate program and charged graduate tuition.
Eligible for: Private donor scholarships
Missionaries & Deferral
Toggle Item
As a missionary, when do I need to apply for scholarships?
Before your mission:
Apply for scholarships before you leave on your mission as a freshman, even if you plan to defer for your mission before attending BYU.
Returning from your mission:
If you have completed at least 12 BYU credits, you will automatically be considered for merit-based scholarships when returning from your mission - no application needed. This only applies to the scholarship award period right after your mission.
For future years, you will need to apply again
.
Examples of scholarship consideration:
You return fall 2026
Your automatic scholarship consideration applies for fall 2026 and winter 2027.
You will need to apply for scholarships again for spring 2027, summer 2027, fall 2027, and winter 2028 by the
January 2, 2027
deadline
.
You return winter 2027
Automatic consideration applies to winter 2027.
You will need to apply for scholarships again for spring 2027, summer 2027, fall 2027, and winter 2028 by the
January 2, 2027 deadline
.
NOTE:
If you have not completed 12 BYU credit hours, you will not be eligible for a new merit-based award when you return. However, if you were awarded a scholarship before your mission, it should have been deferred, and you can use it when you return to BYU.
To be considered for private donor or need-based scholarships:
We encourage you to complete the scholarship application and FAFSA to expand your opportunities. In this case, the regular scholarship deadline will apply.
NOTE:
If you apply for a semester/term you will not attend (per your mission deferment), you will not be considered for that scholarship cycle.
Toggle Item
Can return missionaries apply for Cougar Pell Promise/Cougar Pell Elevated?
No, you can only be considered for Cougar Pell Promise/Cougar Pell Elevated when applying during your senior year of high school.
Even if you plan to serve a mission before attending BYU, you should apply by the January 2 scholarship deadline during your senior year of high school
.

BYU Marriott Scholarships: Important Dates -- BYU Marriott School of Business scholarships and funding.
The scholarship application
opens on December 1, 2025 at 7:00 a.m. MST.

name: BYU Marriott Scholarships: Who Should Apply?
text:
BYU Marriott Scholarships: Who Should Apply? -- BYU Marriott School of Business scholarships and funding.
Full-time undergraduate students officially admitted to BYU Marriott
All MAcc and MISM students as well as continuing MBA, MPA, and EMPA students
Students in any BYU Marriott program interested in applying for the entrepreneurship scholarships
Students applying for the single parent scholarship

name: BYU Marriott Scholarships: Who Should Not Apply?
text:
BYU Marriott Scholarships: Who Should Not Apply? -- BYU Marriott School of Business scholarships and funding.
Students who should not apply include:
Part-time students
Students minoring in business management
Students leaving on missions
EMBA students
All incoming MBA students (please see
MBA Financial Aid & Scholarships
)
CIS recipients

name: BYU Marriott Scholarships: What Scholarships are Available?
text:
BYU Marriott Scholarships: What Scholarships are Available? -- BYU Marriott School of Business scholarships and funding.
BYU Marriott Scholarships
Privately funded scholarships are available for undergraduate and graduate BYU Marriott students. The following criteria may be considered: GPA, financial need, service, leadership, hometown, major, experience, first generation, etc.
Program and Emphasis Specific
Program-specific scholarships may be awarded to qualified students in each of the following undergraduate programs: accounting, entrepreneurship, ExDM, finance, global supply chain, human resources, information systems, and marketing. General undergraduate scholarships are also awarded regardless of BYU Marriott program.
Scholarships are offered to students enrolled in the following graduate programs: MAcc, MBA, MISM, MPA, and EMPA. Emphasis-specific scholarships may be offered to MBA students in entrepreneurship, finance, global supply chain, human resources, or marketing.
BYU Marriott Single Parent
BYU Marriott has several scholarships specifically donated by the Spouse Association of the school’s National Advisory Council to help single-parent graduate BYU Marriott students with educational expenses. In order to be considered for this scholarship, the following conditions must be met:
Applicant is a single parent (mother or father) with custody of dependent child(ren).
Applicant must be officially admitted to one of the graduate programs at BYU Marriott.
Student must remain in good academic standing and will be reevaluated yearly.
When a student gets married, he/she is no longer eligible for the scholarship.
Entrepreneurship
The Rollins Center for Entrepreneurship and Technology sponsors scholarships for students who have demonstrated an interest in and aptitude for entrepreneurship. The following criteria may be considered: GPA; financial need; major, minor, and/or emphasis; courses taken in entrepreneurship; businesses you have created or are creating; involvement in entrepreneurial clubs or associations; and participation in programs and activities sponsored by the center.
BYU Management Society
Scholarships are sponsored by various chapters of the
BYU Management Society
around the country. Recipients must be from the chapter location, so please make certain that your hometown location is correct on the personal information page of the scholarship application.

name: BYU Marriott Scholarships: Graduate Student Scholarships
text:
BYU Marriott Scholarships: Graduate Student Scholarships -- BYU Marriott School of Business scholarships and funding.
Please note the following guidelines:
MAcc: First-year MAcc students are considered undergraduate students for the purpose of scholarships.
MISM: First-year MISM students are considered undergraduate students for the purpose of scholarships.
MBA: Incoming MBA students can find more information
here
. All returning MBA students need to apply
here
.
MPA: All MPA students need to apply online to be considered for scholarships.
Definition of a Graduate Student
Graduate students are defined as students who are paying graduate tuition. MAcc and MISM students who are still paying undergraduate tuition are undergraduates in the scholarship system.

name: BYU Marriott Scholarships: Study Abroad Financial Aid
text:
BYU Marriott Scholarships: Study Abroad Financial Aid -- BYU Marriott School of Business scholarships and funding.
Opportunities for financial aid assistance for study abroad programs are managed through the Whitmore Global Business Center. If you would like to apply for study abroad financial assistance, please click
here
.

name: BYU Marriott Scholarships: BYU Student Scholarships
text:
BYU Marriott Scholarships: BYU Student Scholarships -- BYU Marriott School of Business scholarships and funding.
These are awarded by the university to undergraduates only. Apply online for BYU student scholarships through the
University Scholarship Office
.

name: Find an Opportunity
text:
Find an Opportunity -- BYU experiential learning and academic internships (Experience BYU).
Meet With Your Internship Coordinator
July 08, 2019 02:02 PM
Get personalized guidance on finding internship opportunities, preparing your application, and navigating the internship process.
Meet With A Career Director
Work with a career professional to develop your career plan, strengthen your job search strategy, and prepare for future opportunities.
Internship Provider Database
Explore a database of organizations offering internship experiences and connect with potential employers.
Search Opportunities On Handshake
Find and apply for internships, jobs, and career opportunities posted specifically for students.
Attend Fairs and Events
Meet employers, learn about opportunities, and build connections through career fairs and networking events.
Network With Alumni on BYU Connect
Connect with BYU alumni to gain career insights, advice, and potential internship leads.
Search BYU Postings On Y Jobs
Discover on-campus and BYU-affiliated job and internship opportunities available to students.
Find Internship Experiences Through Y Serve
Gain valuable experience through service-based internships and community engagement opportunities.

name: Next Steps
text:
Next Steps -- BYU experiential learning and academic internships (Experience BYU).
Add Class
After your internship application is fully approved by your Department Internship Coordinator and Experiential Learning & Internships, you will receive an email directing you to add the internship course to your schedule through AIM or MyMap.
Make Sure Everything is in Order
Be sure to add the internship course to your schedule before you begin to count your internship work hours and review the internship course syllabus.
Go & Do!
Participate in the internship experience, complete course assignments, keep track of your internship work hours, and stay in contact with your department internship coordinator and faculty mentor. (link to future-focused approach)

name: Dates & Deadlines
text:
Dates & Deadlines -- BYU experiential learning and academic internships (Experience BYU).
Spring 2026
Spr/Sum 2026
Summer 2026
Fall 2026
Priority Registration
Mar 1, 2026
Jun 1, 2026
Class Begins
Apr 28, 2026
Jun 22, 2026
Sep 2, 2026
Add/Drop
May 5, 2026
Jun 29, 2026
Sep 10, 2026
Withdrawal deadline
(last day to add an internship course)
Jun 2, 2026
Jun 15, 2026
Jul 28, 2026
Nov 24, 2026
Other Registration Dates and Deadlines
Adding an Internship Course After the Add/Drop Deadline
Students whose internship applications are approved by the department internship coordinator and Experiential Learning and Internships after the add/drop deadline and prior to the discontinuance deadline will be automatically added into the internship course by the Records and Registration Office. Once the student’s internship application is fully approved, the Internship Registration and Management System (IRAMS) will generate and send an automatic email to the Registration Office team directing them to add the internship course to the student’s schedule. The IRAMS generated email will include specific information included in the student’s internship application. Internship coordinators need to be precise with the internship course number and section, and the number of internship credits approved in the internship application since that is the information that will be used by the Registration Office to add the internship course to the student’s schedule.
Change the Number of Credits After the Add/Drop Deadline
If you have a student who is enrolled in an internship course, and would like to change the number of credits will require you to send an email to the Registration Office. Please send the email to
registration@byu.edu
including the information listed below, if the discontinuance deadline has not passed.
Please include the following in the email sent to
registration@byu.edu
.
Internship coordinator Name
Internship program/department
Student name
Student BYU ID
Current semester/term
Internship course name, number and section
Number of credit hours the class enrollment should be changed to
If it is past the discontinuance deadline, the student will need to complete on online petition process found on the
Registration Office
website.

name: International Forms Instructions
text:
International Forms Instructions -- BYU experiential learning and academic internships (Experience BYU).
International Internship Agreement
International Individual Internship Proposal Form
Photo by Jaren Wilkey/BYU
Procedure
Ideally, all paperwork will be completed and processed by the Experiential Learning & Internships office prior to the student leaving for the internship. Due to the cultural and linguistical differences in working with International Internship Providers, the process may be delayed. However, every internship that involves international travel must have the appropriate university clearances. The International Study Program will initiate the International Internship Agreement/Memorandum of Understanding with the Internship Provider as part of their international internship approval process. Students and internship coordinators may function as facilitators if asked y the ISP.
International Internship Memorandum of Understanding
The International Internship Agreement/Memorandum of Understanding
is to be filled out by the Internship Provider and signed on page two by an "Administrator or Responsible Officer." In most cases, the department or ISP will initiate this process. When the Internship Provider returns the agreement, it should be forwarded to the Experiential Learning & Internships office for processing. The university requires one agreement per Internship Provider. To see which internship providers have already signed a domestic internship agreement or an international internship agreement, please check the
Internship Agreement Database
. The International Internship Memorandum of Understanding is available in French, German, Chinese, Japanese, Korean and Spanish.
Processing
Please email signed International Internship Agreement/Memorandum of Understanding to experience@byu.edu. The agreement will be executed, entered into the Internship Agreement database, and emailed to the internship provider. If departments would like to receive a copy of the executed agreement, please include the name of the academic unit offering the internship course.

name: Partnering Hub
text:
Partnering Hub -- BYU experiential learning and academic internships (Experience BYU).
The project may not begin until after the ExL Sponsor Agreement has been executed by both BYU and the ExL Sponsor.
Experiential Learning Sponsored Project Agreement Queue
To request an ExL Sponsored Project Agreement, click here
Click here for larger image
Partnering Coordinating Offices (when applicable)
Research Administration Office
For projects that involve: student research from a government sponsor, or research involving human or animal subjects, lasers, and bio hazardous material, faculty will coordinate with the Research Administration Office (RAO) for approval.
Intellectual Property Services
When the sponsored project results in a BYU employee or student invention, a BYU trademark, or a video/music/textbook or other creative work, Intellectual Property Services will assist with securing appropriate licensing, patents, copyrights, trademarks, etc.
Grants and Contracts Accounting
Grants and Contracts Accounting manages the funding, account creation, and reporting for projects approved by the Research Administration Office.
Off-campus Receivables
When funding is not donated, faculty will work with Off-campus Receivables to coordinate payments.
BYU Scholarships
When project funding will be used for student scholarships, stipends, tuition, or grants, the department submits a tuition request to BYU Scholarships to coordinate payments.
Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints Philanthropies
When the partner is donating funding to support a project, without an expected deliverable or contract, the funding is facilitated through Philanthropies.
Non-Disclosure Agreement Template (NDA)
Experiential Learning Sponsor Agreement Template
Request Experiential Learning Sponsor Agreement

name: Submitting Your Experiential Learning Program Evaluation
text:
Submitting Your Experiential Learning Program Evaluation -- BYU experiential learning and academic internships (Experience BYU).
Where can I find and complete my program evaluation?
Go to:
https://byu-csm.symplicity.com
This link will take you to a page where it asks you what type of user you are. Select "Students".
The next window will ask you which sign-in method you would like to use. Select "Current Students: Single Sign On".
After successfully logging in using your BYU credentials, you will see your experiential learning dashboard.
Click on the "Experiential Learning" tab.
You will see all of your experiences listed alphabetically. Locate the correct experience and select "Program Evaluation" (it has a pencil icon next to it).
Your program evaluation will open, ready for you to complete according to the instructions on the form.
The evaluation shouldn't take more than ten minutes to complete. Please be thoughtful in your responses, as the information will be returned to your college/department (your name will not be included) to improve experiences for future students.
Click the button for screenshots of the instructions.
Screenshots
